name: Pearson Correlation
description: Direct bivariate Pearson r between indicator and target. Measures linear relationship. No confound control. Use as a first pass on any indicator.
when_to_use: First pass. Always run this. Shows raw linear relationship.

target.description: Target node key: views, keep, or another indicator key

transform_target.description: Transform applied to target values before correlation. log10 recommended for views.

min_n.description: Minimum number of valid video pairs required to run

formula: r = sum((x_i - x_mean)(y_i - y_mean)) / sqrt(sum((x_i-x_mean)^2) * sum((y_i-y_mean)^2))
interpretation: r > 0.5: strong positive. r 0.3-0.5: moderate. r 0.1-0.3: weak. r < 0.1: no signal.

name: Spearman Rank Correlation
description: Non-parametric rank-based correlation. Robust to outliers and non-normal distributions.
when_to_use: Run alongside Pearson. If rho >> r, relationship is monotonic but not linear.
formula: rho = 1 - (6 * sum(d_i^2)) / (n * (n^2 - 1)) where d_i = rank(x_i) - rank(y_i)
interpretation: Same scale as Pearson r. If |rho| >> |r|, nonlinear but monotonic.

name: Partial Correlation
description: Pearson r between indicator and target AFTER controlling for one or more confounds.
when_to_use: When you suspect a confound is inflating the correlation.

outputs.r_partial: Partial correlation after removing confound variance
outputs.r_direct: Direct Pearson r without confound control

formula: r_partial(X,Y|Z) = (r_XY - r_XZ * r_YZ) / sqrt((1 - r_XZ^2)(1 - r_YZ^2))
interpretation: Compare r_partial to r_direct. If r_partial << r_direct, confound was driving the relationship.

name: OLS R2 Contribution
description: Measures how much R2 improves in cross-validated OLS when adding this indicator to a baseline.
when_to_use: After Pearson/Spearman confirm correlation. Tests if signal adds unique predictive power.

outputs.r2_after: Cross-validated R2 after adding this indicator

formula: delta_R2 = mean(CV R2 with feature) - mean(CV R2 without feature), 5-fold CV
interpretation: delta_R2 > 0.02: meaningful. > 0.05: significant. Negative = indicator hurts model.
